title: fragaria virginiana subsp. grayana
intro: Fragaria virginiana subsp. grayana, also known as Gray's wild strawberry, is a perennial plant species native to western North America, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a subspecies of the Fragaria virginiana species. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of salicylic acid and flavonoids. The health benefits of Fragaria virginiana subsp. grayana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a valuable plant for herbal remedies. Fragaria virginiana subsp. grayana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb to utilize its medicinal properties and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effectiveness.
